title: Workspace wizard: create project from missing cwd, later support templates
description:
The dashboard `n` flow should eventually support creating a project from templates, not just empty directories.

Current implementation can create a missing cwd and run `git init`. Follow-up ideas:
- choose from project templates
- bootstrap files after directory creation
- profile-specific templates
- maybe infer template from selected agent/profile

title: Detect agent questions in Stop hook
description:
The Stop hook question detection is implemented but not working. The hook (`extensions/claude/stop-hook.sh`) reads `last_assistant_message` from stdin, checks the last 500 chars for `?`, and should report `question` state. Instead it always reports `idle`.

## Symptoms

- Agent finishes a turn ending with a question mark
- Dashboard shows `idle` instead of `question`
- Stop hook fires but the `?` grep doesn't match

## Debugging

Debug logging added to stop-hook.sh — dumps raw input to `/tmp/claude-stop-hook-debug.json` and parsed message to `/tmp/claude-stop-hook-msg.txt`. Check these after the next Stop hook invocation to see what `last_assistant_message` actually contains.

## Possible causes

1. `last_assistant_message` field is empty or missing from the Stop hook JSON
2. The message is in a different format than expected (e.g. structured blocks, not plain text)
3. `jq` is not available or fails silently
4. The `?` is outside the last 500 chars (unlikely for short responses)

## Related

The Notification hook with `elicitation_dialog` matcher correctly reports `question` for interactive prompts (AskUserQuestion tool). This bug is specifically about detecting questions in free-text assistant output.

title: Auto-create tmux session when launched outside tmux
description:
If `agents` is launched outside of a tmux session (no $TMUX env var), it should automatically:

1. Create a new tmux session (e.g. `tmux new-session -d -s agents`)
2. Launch `agents watch` inside that session
3. Attach to the session

This makes `agents` a single entry point — just type `agents` from any terminal and you get the dashboard in tmux, ready to create workspaces and monitor agents.

## Implementation

At the top of cli.ts, before command parsing:
- Check if `process.env.TMUX` is set
- If not, exec into `tmux new-session -s agents 'agents watch'` (or attach if session already exists)
- Use `execSync`/`execvp` style replacement so the original process is replaced

## Edge cases
- If a tmux `agents` session already exists, attach to it instead of creating a new one
- If tmux is not installed, print a helpful error
- Pass through any args: `agents ls` outside tmux should still just print and exit (only the dashboard needs tmux)

title: Launch profiles in config
Session-restorable workspaces (tmux-resurrect / zellij)
description:
Workspace panes created by `agents ws` are lost on tmux server restart because commands are launched via `send-keys` — tmux-resurrect can't see the original command.

## Goals
- Workspaces should survive tmux-resurrect restore (or native Zellij session restore)
- No dependency on personal scripts (like tmux-run)

## Approaches to explore

### tmux-resurrect compatible
- Use tmux remain-on-exit + a wrapper script shipped with agents
- Or set pane commands directly (not send-keys) with a shell wrapper that sources rc files first

### Zellij support
- Zellij has native session restore and start_suspended for panes
- Could generate a Zellij layout file from workspace config
- Would need a parallel code path in workspace.ts (detect zellij vs tmux)

### Shared
- Workspace config is already multiplexer-agnostic (split directions, sizes, commands)
- The abstraction layer is the workspace definition — generation backend could be pluggable

title: Detect sub-agent relationships in dashboard
description:
Detect when agents spawn background sub-agents and surface the relationship in the dashboard.

## Detection approaches

### Process tree (universal, no extension needed)
The scanner already walks process trees via findLeafProcess. Detect agent→agent parent-child chains (e.g. pi spawning claude). Tag these as sub-agents and show nested in dashboard.

### Pi extension hooks (richest data)
Pi's @tintinweb/pi-subagents provides Agent/get_subagent_result/steer_subagent tools. Hook into tool_call events to detect delegation:
```typescript
pi.on("tool_call", async (event) => {
  if (event.tool === "Agent") report("delegating");
});
```

### Claude
No tool-level hook events available. Fall back to process tree or screen-scrape for Agent tool patterns.

### Copilot
SDK sub-agent support unclear. Process tree fallback.

## Dashboard UX
- Show sub-agents as children of parent agent
- Parent status could show "delegating" or "working (2 sub-agents)"
- Clicking parent could expand/collapse sub-agent list

title: Make 'agents' default to dashboard, move list to 'agents ls'
description:
Currently `agents` (no subcommand) defaults to the interactive list picker (`agents list`). The dashboard (`agents watch`) is the primary UI and should be the default.

## Changes

- `agents` with no args → launch `agents watch` (dashboard)
- `agents ls` / `agents list` → current behavior (one-shot list, interactive picker)
- `agents watch` still works as explicit alias

In commander terms: change `isDefault: true` from the `list` command to the `watch` command.

title: Create agent from dashboard UI
description:
Add the ability to launch new agent workspaces directly from the dashboard without dropping to a shell.

## Proposed UX

**Trigger**: Press `n` (new) in the dashboard.

**Flow**:
1. A compact picker appears inline (replaces the hotkey bar), showing available launch profiles (see bd-1fw)
2. The picker header shows the inherited context: session name, cwd (shortened), and selected agent name — e.g. `dustbot ~/code/agents (claude)`
3. Navigate with j/k or arrow keys, confirm with Enter, cancel with Esc
4. On confirm: runs `agents ws <profile>` under the hood — creates a new tmux window with the agent + helper panes per the workspace layout config
5. The new agent appears in the dashboard list on the next scan refresh
6. Optionally auto-select and preview the new agent

**Minimal v1**: Skip the picker if there's only one profile — just launch it immediately on `n`.

## Context inheritance

The new workspace inherits from the currently selected agent in the list:
- **cwd**: Use the selected agent's pane working directory (`tmux display-message -t <paneId> -p '#{pane_current_path}'`)
- **tmux session/window**: Create the new window in the same tmux session as the selected agent

This is the natural UX — you're looking at an agent working on a project and want to spin up another one in the same context. If no agent is selected or the list is empty, fall back to the dashboard's own session and cwd.

## Edge cases
- If no profiles are configured, show a hint: "Configure launch profiles in ~/.agents/config.json"
- Dashboard stays focused in its own pane throughout (workspace creation happens in a new tmux window)

Depends on launch profiles (bd-1fw) being implemented first.

title: Zellij: break_panes_to_tab_with_index position/ID mismatch after tab deletion
description:
After a tab is deleted in Zellij 0.44, break_panes_to_tab_with_index validates by tab position but inserts by tab ID. These diverge after deletion, causing panes to land in wrong tabs or vanish entirely. This blocks side-by-side preview (swapping agent pane next to dashboard). Workaround: use break_panes_to_new_tab instead, but that creates a new tab every time rather than targeting an existing one.

Upstream: https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/issues/3535 (open since Aug 2024, confirmed through 0.42.1+)

title: Zellij: resize_pane_with_id doesn't work on panes moved via break_panes_to_new_tab
description:
In Zellij 0.44, calling resize_pane_with_id on a pane that was moved via break_panes_to_new_tab has no effect. The plugin API resize silently fails. CLI resize (zellij action resize --pane-id) works as a workaround but requires a feedback loop (up to 30 iterations checking actual width) to achieve deterministic sizing. This blocks reliable side-by-side preview layout.

Upstream: NOT YET FILED — could not find an existing issue on zellij-org/zellij. Should file one with a minimal repro.
